accurate.why: All claims match the bill text (Section 547(f) amendment, figures, fiscal years) and the added context about the BCOR program supporting substance use disorder recovery is verified as accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: The contextual description of the grant program assisting individuals recovering from substance use disorders is accurate real-world context, not fabrication.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly states the bill reauthorizes a grant program for building communities of recovery by amending the Public Health Service Act.

changes_to_existing_law.why: It correctly identifies the amendment to Section 547(f) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 290ee-2(f)).

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions, conditions, or carve-outs to summarize.

effective_dates.why: The summary notes the FY2026-2030 authorization period and the April 7, 2025 introduction date.

major_provisions_covered.why: It captures the sole operative effect: striking the old funding authorization and inserting the new amount and fiscal years.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing or advocacy.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized, consistent, and reads clearly.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately brief without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: The Public Health Service Act, Section 547(f), the citation, and the Committee on Energy and Commerce are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: All figures are correct: $5,000,000 for FY2019-2023 replaced by $17,000,000 for FY2026-2030, and the April 7, 2025 date.
